family_title: Ask for simpler words
family_summary: When speed is not the only problem, ask for easier wording instead of the same sentence again.
context: Use this after 'I don't understand' when the problem is the wording, not just the speed.
search_aliases: simpler words|say it more simply|easy words
notes: Phase 1A premium repair scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Move the repair to their phone
family_summary: Typing often rescues names, addresses, and booking details faster than repeating them out loud.
english_text: Can you type it on your phone?
context: Use this when spelling or repeating still is not enough.
search_aliases: type it on your phone|type it|show me typed
notes: Phase 1A premium repair scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask them to send it as a message
family_summary: A text message can rescue addresses, pickup details, and booking references without more confusion.
english_text: Can you text it to me?
context: Use this when you need the address, name, or instructions in a form you can show later.
search_aliases: text it to me|send me a message|send it by text
notes: Phase 1A premium repair scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask them to spell the name
family_summary: This is for hotel names, bus companies, streets, and people whose names you need exactly.
english_text: Can you spell the name for me?
context: Use this when the key thing is a name you need to recognize or copy correctly.
search_aliases: spell the name|spell it for me|name spelling
notes: Phase 1A premium repair scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Clarify the exit or doorway
family_summary: This prevents getting lost when the place is right but the exact entrance, gate, or exit is still unclear.
context: Use this at stations, malls, airports, or big buildings when the destination is nearby but not solved.
search_aliases: which exit|which gate|which entrance
notes: Phase 1A premium repair scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for the exact time
family_summary: This fixes vague timing before it becomes a missed ride, check-in, or pickup.
context: Use this when 'later' or 'around then' is not precise enough for the situation.
search_aliases: what time exactly|exact time|which time exactly
notes: Phase 1A premium repair scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Challenge a wrong total
family_summary: Use this when the number in front of you does not match what you expected to pay.
context: Use this at the register, on a receipt, or on a payment screen before the charge goes through.
search_aliases: wrong total|total is wrong|this total is wrong
notes: Phase 1A premium billing scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask why the price changed
family_summary: This is for the moment when the quoted amount and the final amount do not match.
english_text: Why is the price different now?
context: Use this when the price changed after you already understood the earlier number.
search_aliases: price changed|different price now|why is it different
notes: Phase 1A premium billing scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask what the extra fee is for
family_summary: This keeps the conversation focused on the exact charge instead of broad disagreement.
context: Use this when you see an added fee, service charge, or extra line that was not clear before.
search_aliases: what fee is this|extra fee|what is this charge
notes: Phase 1A premium billing scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask them to write the total down
family_summary: Written numbers lower the chance of agreeing to the wrong amount by mistake.
context: Use this when speech or noise is making the amount hard to confirm safely.
search_aliases: write the total down|write total|show total in writing
notes: Phase 1A premium billing scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Check whether service is already included
family_summary: This prevents tipping and fee confusion from getting worse after the first number looks settled.
context: Use this before you pay when you are not sure whether service or another charge is already built in.
search_aliases: service included|is service included|already included
notes: Phase 1A premium billing scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask to split the payment
family_summary: This is a cost-control follow-up for shared rides, meals, and checkout moments.
context: Use this when two people need separate payment instead of one combined total.
search_aliases: split the payment|pay separately|split bill
notes: Phase 1A premium billing scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say that this is not your car
family_summary: This helps you stop the interaction early before a wrong pickup turns into a bigger mistake.
context: Use this when the plate, driver, or app details do not match the ride you expected.
search_aliases: not my car|wrong car|wrong driver
notes: Phase 1A premium transport scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Confirm the pickup point
family_summary: Pickup confusion is one of the fastest ways for a ride to go sideways in a crowded area.
english_text: Is this the right pickup point?
context: Use this when the driver and rider are nearby but not clearly in the same place yet.
search_aliases: right pickup point|pickup point|is this where you pick up
notes: Phase 1A premium transport scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Keep the driver at the pickup
family_summary: This buys you a little time when the car arrived but you are still crossing, walking, or exiting the building.
english_text: Please wait here, I am coming.
context: Use this when the ride is about to leave or circle away before you reach it.
search_aliases: wait here|i am coming|please wait for me here
notes: Phase 1A premium transport scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say the route is wrong
family_summary: This is the direct premium recovery sentence for when the car is heading the wrong way.
context: Use this as soon as you realize the driver is not following the correct route or destination.
search_aliases: route is wrong|wrong route|this is the wrong way
notes: Phase 1A premium transport scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Tell the driver to turn around
family_summary: When a wrong turn already happened, you need a correction sentence, not just a complaint.
context: Use this when the driver needs to reverse direction instead of continuing ahead.
search_aliases: turn around|go back|please go back
notes: Phase 1A premium transport scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Stop the car before your luggage leaves with it
family_summary: This is the recovery line for the moment right after arrival when the trunk still matters.
english_text: My luggage is in the trunk.
context: Use this before the driver pulls away if your bag is still in the trunk or back of the vehicle.
search_aliases: luggage in the trunk|my bag is in the trunk|bag in trunk
notes: Phase 1A premium transport scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Flag a booking problem early
family_summary: This is the opening sentence for mismatches before you start explaining the details.
english_text: I think there is a problem with my booking.
context: Use this when the desk cannot find the booking or the reservation details do not match.
search_aliases: problem with my booking|booking problem|reservation problem
notes: Phase 1A premium hotel scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say that you booked a different room
family_summary: This keeps the dispute focused on the room type instead of a broad complaint.
context: Use this when the bed type, room type, or booking details do not match what you reserved.
search_aliases: different room|booked another room|wrong room type
notes: Phase 1A premium hotel scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Push on a room that still is not ready
family_summary: This covers the recovery moment when check-in stalled and you need a clear next step.
english_text: The room is not ready yet.
context: Use this when you were told to wait but the room still is not available when it should be.
search_aliases: room not ready|not ready yet|still not ready
notes: Phase 1A premium hotel scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Report that there is no hot water
family_summary: This is a common room-recovery problem and a believable premium follow-up beyond basic check-in.
context: Use this when the shower or sink water is cold and you need the hotel to fix it.
search_aliases: no hot water|hot water not working|cold shower
notes: Phase 1A premium hotel scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Say the room is too noisy
family_summary: Noise is a real hotel failure and often needs a room move or staff action.
context: Use this when outside noise, neighbors, or hotel noise is making the room unusable.
search_aliases: room too noisy|too noisy|noise problem
notes: Phase 1A premium hotel scaffold, not live yet

family_title: Ask for a later checkout
family_summary: This is a practical hotel recovery sentence when timing on the last day gets tighter than expected.
context: Use this when your ride, flight, or day plan makes the normal checkout time too early.
search_aliases: late checkout|check out later|later checkout
notes: Phase 1A premium hotel scaffold, not live yet
